values.title: Forge & Fabricate Makerspace
values.hero_description: A community workshop where makers, tinkerers, artists, and inventors come together to create. Access professional-grade tools, learn new skills, and bring your ideas to life.
values.cta_description: Tour the space, meet our community, and discover what you can create. First-time visitors get a free day pass to explore all our workshops.

values.title: Introduction to Woodworking
values.body:
Learn the fundamentals of woodworking in this hands-on beginner workshop. You will learn to safely operate the table saw, miter saw, drill press, and hand tools while building a solid wood cutting board to take home.

What You Will Learn

  • Wood selection and grain direction
  • Safe operation of power tools
  • Measuring, marking, and cutting techniques
  • Gluing, clamping, and finishing

All materials are included. No experience necessary. Closed-toe shoes required.

values.title: 3D Printing Fundamentals
values.body:
Demystify 3D printing from design to finished object. This workshop covers 3D modeling basics in TinkerCAD and Fusion 360, slicer settings, filament selection, and machine operation. You will design and print your own creation to take home.

Topics Covered

  • Overview of FDM and resin printing
  • 3D modeling with TinkerCAD (beginner-friendly)
  • Slicing software and print settings
  • Troubleshooting common print failures
  • Post-processing and finishing techniques

Bring a laptop if you have one (we have loaners available).

values.title: MIG Welding Basics
values.body:
Get hands-on with MIG welding in this introductory workshop. Learn proper safety procedures, machine setup, and fundamental welding techniques. By the end of class, you will be running consistent beads and completing simple joint welds.

What to Expect

  • Safety gear fitting and PPE review
  • Machine anatomy and setup
  • Flat position welding practice
  • Butt joints and T-joints
  • Grinding and finishing

All safety equipment and materials provided. Long pants and closed-toe boots required. Minimum age 16.

values.title: CO2 Laser Cutter
values.body:
Our 60W CO2 laser cutter handles precision cutting and engraving on wood, acrylic, leather, fabric, paper, and more. The 24x36 inch bed accommodates a wide range of project sizes, from jewelry to signage.

Capabilities

  • Cuts wood up to 1/4 inch, acrylic up to 3/8 inch
  • High-detail engraving at up to 1000 DPI
  • Built-in camera for precise alignment
  • Compatible with SVG, DXF, and bitmap files

Safety

Certification workshop required before first use. The laser cutter must never be left unattended while running.

values.title: CNC Router
values.body:
A full-size 4x8 foot CNC router for precision milling of wood, plastics, foam, and soft metals. Ideal for furniture components, signage, prototypes, and artistic pieces.

Specifications

  • 48 x 96 inch cutting area
  • 3 HP spindle, variable speed
  • Vacuum hold-down table
  • Dust collection system
  • Compatible with VCarve, Fusion 360, and other CAM software

Requirements

CNC certification workshop required. Members must submit toolpath files for review before first independent use.

values.title: 3D Printers
values.body:
A fleet of six 3D printers covering both FDM and resin technologies. From rapid prototyping to high-detail miniatures, our 3D printing lab has the right machine for your project.

Available Printers

  • Prusa MK4 (4x): Reliable FDM printing in PLA, PETG, ABS, and flexibles
  • Bambu Lab X1 Carbon (1x): Multi-material FDM with carbon fiber capability
  • Formlabs Form 3+ (1x): High-detail SLA resin printing

Getting Started

A brief orientation is required for first-time users. Filament is available for purchase at cost. Resin printing uses a per-mL usage fee.

values.title: Mini Maker Faire
values.body:
Our annual Mini Maker Faire is the biggest event of the year! Local makers, artists, and inventors showcase their projects, run live demonstrations, and inspire the next generation of creators. Free admission and activities for all ages.

What to Expect

  • 50+ maker exhibits and demonstrations
  • Hands-on activity stations for kids
  • Live 3D printing and laser cutting demos
  • Robot battles and drone racing
  • Food trucks and maker market

image.alt: Maker faire with exhibits and demonstrations

values.title: Monthly Open House
values.body:
Curious about the makerspace? Join us for our monthly open house. Tour the facility, meet current members, try out some tools, and ask any questions about membership. Refreshments provided.

No registration needed. Just drop in anytime between 2 and 5 PM.

values.title: 24-Hour Hardware Hackathon
values.body:
Design, build, and present a working hardware prototype in just 24 hours. Teams of 2-4 compete for prizes using any tools and materials in the makerspace. Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and electronic components provided.

Prizes

  • 1st Place: $500 and 6-month membership
  • 2nd Place: $250 and 3-month membership
  • Best Design: 3-month membership

Registration required. $25 per person ($15 for members) includes meals and snacks.

values.title: About Forge & Fabricate
values.body:
Forge & Fabricate Makerspace opened in 2018 as a community workshop where anyone can access professional-grade tools and learn hands-on skills. What started with a few woodworking tools in a garage has grown into an 8,000 square foot facility with over 75 machines and tools.

Our Mission

To democratize access to tools, technology, and knowledge so that everyone has the power to design, build, repair, and create.

Workshop Areas

  • Woodshop: Table saw, band saw, planer, jointer, router table, hand tools
  • Metal Shop: MIG and TIG welders, plasma cutter, grinders, drill press
  • Digital Fab Lab: Laser cutter, CNC router, 3D printers, vinyl cutter
  • Electronics Bench: Soldering stations, oscilloscopes, power supplies, components
  • Textile Studio: Sewing machines, serger, screen printing, heat press

values.title: Membership Information
values.body:
Membership Plans

We offer flexible membership options to fit your making needs.

  • Day Pass ($25): Full access for one day. Perfect for trying us out.
  • Basic ($75/month): Open hours access, 10% workshop discount
  • Standard ($125/month): 24/7 access, 20% workshop discount, storage locker
  • Family ($175/month): Standard benefits for up to 4 household members

What is Included

All memberships include access to shared tools and equipment, WiFi, and community events. Certification workshops for restricted equipment are discounted for members.

Safety Requirements

All new members complete a safety orientation before using the space. Equipment-specific certifications are required for laser cutters, CNC machines, welding equipment, and other high-risk tools.
